flags.defaults_note: all remaining llama-server options at 8f114a9 build defaults (same process as the chat-short calibration probe)

model.tokenizer: gguf-embedded (Qwen2 BPE as packaged in the checkpoint; identity covered by the model-file sha256 above)

hardware.instance_type: local-dev-host (linux/amd64, 4 CPU cores, 15 GiB RAM; llama-server -t 4, infergate, and inferbench co-located on the same cores)

gateway.version: 74f2372 (infergate@74f2372acea62645fa3c1d91689574ea9de7c589, host binary built read-only via git archive; binary sha256 in evidence/i3/logs/build-info.txt)
gateway.config_version: v1-24d2072b (scenarios/b/config/gateway-llamacpp.json: llamacpp backend, no fallback, upstream_timeout 300s, stream_write_timeout 30s)

client.location: same host (inferbench on host -> infergate 127.0.0.1:18080 -> llama-server 127.0.0.1:18082, loopback; client shares the 4 CPU cores with the engine)

hypothesis: SLO calibration probe (long-prompt / cold-prefill regime): the composed inferbench -> infergate -> llama-server path completes this 8-request seeded shared-prefix schedule at the CPU-adapted 0.04 rps with error+shed rate 0; the run's measured per-request maxima (TTFT, e2e, max stall -- including decode stalls caused by a concurrent slot's prefill ubatch) seed the scenario SLO thresholds (x1.5 headroom, rounded up -- see the generated evidence/i3/raw/slo/ file and checks.log derive-slo entry).
notes: Calibration/warm-up probe, not a benchmark: no warm-up exclusion, seed deliberately different from the measured runs (2003004).
